global_context.scene_description: A photorealistic split-frame composition showing the SAME homeowner (white woman, mid-30s, casual clothes, brown hair, face cropped or angled away from camera so identity isn't focal) in two contrasting states. The image is split vertically down the middle. LEFT HALF: she sits at a cluttered kitchen table, head in her hands, elbows down, body language tense and stressed. The table is covered in scattered HVAC quote papers, a calculator, an unopened bill envelope, a half-empty coffee cup. The lighting is harsh overhead fluorescent, slightly blue-grey, anxious. RIGHT HALF: same woman, now relaxed on a cream-colored couch, feet up on an ottoman, holding a fresh hot coffee mug, head tilted slightly back as if exhaling relief. Soft warm late-afternoon light pours from a window. The room is calm, cool, lived-in. Both halves of the image are visually distinct in lighting and color but clearly the same woman, same wardrobe (light denim, cream sweater). The split is VISIBLE — a clean hard line down the middle, like before/after photos. No text overlays. The visual story: dread to relief, after the AC decision was easier than feared. Photorealistic, shot on 35mm lens.
global_context.time_of_day: left: harsh midday | right: soft late afternoon

lighting.source: left: overhead fluorescent | right: window light
lighting.quality: left: cold harsh | right: warm soft

composition.focal_point: the contrast between the woman's two postures
